vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The visual data indicates a severe discrepancy between the registry and the physical reality. The pots are present, but the biological occupants are largely absent or non-viable.

inventory_reconciliation.P3_Pothos: Systemic Loss: Only a single, isolated, possibly necrotic stem remains.

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil surface; no signs of succulent growth.
plant_audit.P2: Empty soil surface; no signs of mint growth.
plant_audit.P3: Contains the white rabbit anchor; one thin, vertical green shoot is visible, but it appears isolated and lacks the structural complexity of a Pothos.
plant_audit.P4: Merged with P2/P3 space; no distinct specimen identified.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Appears dark and potentially saturated, but lacks any organic growth.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Clean, no debris or fungal colonies noted.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_current: The sequence shows a consistent state of biological vacancy. There is no evidence of growth, recovery, or change in the state of the soil or the single remaining shoot over the 5-day observation period.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of total collapse. The lack of photosynthetic material suggests the specimens have either been removed or have undergone complete senescence/death prior to the start of this observation window.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of a single, unidentified green shoot in P3 is the only biological anomaly. The 'White Rabbit' anchor is the only consistent element across the timeline.
vision_report.narrative_description: Upon meticulous audit of the provided image stack, it is clear that the expected botanical specimens (P1-P4) are not present in any viable form. The pots contain only soil and the white rabbit anchor. The single green shoot observed in P3 is insufficient to constitute a healthy Pothos specimen and may represent a remnant or an opportunistic weed. The biome is effectively dormant or dead.
